name: Metlo API Security Platform API
what_it_does: Open-source API security platform with REST API — automatically discovering API endpoints by analyzing network traffic, testing APIs for OWASP vulnerabilities (injection, broken auth, excessive data exposure), detecting sensitive data in API responses, and providing a security-focused API catalog.
use_cases: Automatically discovering undocumented API endpoints by mirroring production traffic
Testing API endpoints for OWASP API Top 10 vulnerabilities via automated scans
Detecting PII and sensitive data in API responses to prevent data exposure
Querying the API catalog for endpoint inventory and security posture reporting
Integrating Metlo security findings into CI/CD pipelines via API for shift-left security
not_for: Runtime WAF and API blocking (Metlo is detection, not prevention)
Teams without traffic mirroring or agent deployment capability
best_when: A security or platform engineering team wants open-source API discovery, vulnerability testing, and sensitive data detection without paying enterprise WAF prices, deployable in their own infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need real-time API blocking and rate limiting (use Kong or AWS API Gateway), or your API count is small and manually documentable.

auth.notes: API key from Metlo dashboard. Passed as X-API-Key header. Self-hosted deployment (open-source) or Metlo Cloud (managed). No OAuth2. Traffic mirroring agent requires API key for data upload. Self-hosted version gives full control over auth setup.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open-source self-hosted: unlimited; Cloud: free tier available
pricing.paid_tiers: Metlo Cloud: contact sales for enterprise
pricing.notes: Open-source (Apache 2.0) on GitHub. YC W22 company (San Francisco). Founded 2022. Backed by YC and angels. Self-hosted with Docker Compose. Traffic mirroring via AWS VPC Mirroring, GCP packet mirroring, or eBPF agent. Cloud offering for teams without ops capacity.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for endpoints, alerts, sensitive data, and tests. X-API-Key header auth. Good documentation for self-hosted deployment. Traffic mirroring agent setup required before API discovery works. Test suite runs asynchronously. Self-hosted endpoint URL varies per deployment.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All read operations fully idempotent. Endpoint catalog reads idempotent. Alert reads idempotent. Security test results idempotent by test ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Traffic mirroring agent must be deployed before API endpoints are discovered
Self-hosted endpoint URL varies — no fixed cloud URL unless using Metlo Cloud
Security tests are asynchronous — poll for completion
Open-source project — check GitHub for latest stable version before deploying
eBPF agent requires Linux kernel 4.15+ and appropriate permissions

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS configurable (self-hosted). Open-source with community security reviews. Apache 2.0. Self-hosted ensures traffic data never leaves your infrastructure. API key for Metlo dashboard access. Traffic mirroring agent sees API traffic — ensure encrypted transit. YC-backed with active development.
